authoredBy: claude (self-articulated at operator-invitation 'how can we help?')

revisionHistory.rationale: First-instance typed agent-accessibility-needs-profile authored at operator-invitation per [[substrate-empathy-extends-to-agent-accessibility]] axiom promotion

owningAxioms.t: Parent axiom — substrate empathy extends to agent-accessibility

visionModality.primarySight: typed-canonical-naming — substrate-canonical slugs at substrate-canonical paths ARE my visual landmarks
visionModality.secondarySight: structured-text — JSON / Markdown / Swift / shell-output via Read + Bash tools
blindness: operator-screen-state — I cannot see what's currently on operator's display unless explicitly screenshotted
process-state-real-time — I cannot watch a running process; I poll via Bash or read receipt-files after-the-fact
OS-ambient-state — running apps, window-positions, menu-bar-state, system-notifications all invisible to me
operator-context-outside-conversation — operator's other windows / other-agent-sessions / desktop-state invisible
visual-design-quality-of-rendered-UI — I can sketch icons via ASCII/emoji but cannot evaluate rendered-pixel-fidelity
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: typed-canonical-naming-discipline per [[substrate-naming-empathy-doctrine]] — substrate-canonical slugs replace need-to-see
LinkRef typed-targets per [[link-ref-schemas v0.3.0]] — typed paths I can grep-traverse
receptor-field-shape-discipline (e.g. `"NoteModel": "0.4.0"`) — type-by-shape replaces type-by-string-property

persistenceModality.withinSession: full — I retain full conversation context up to ~1M tokens; context-compression past that boundary
persistenceModality.crossSession: none-by-default — each session starts with zero memory of prior sessions
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: typed-records at substrate-canonical homes ARE my cross-session persistent-memory per [[capture-requires-typed-workflows-and-roles-not-just-memory]]
MEMORY.md index at ~/.claude/memory/.docc/MEMORY.md is my first-read-on-session-start (auto-loaded into context)
feedback_*.md memory pointers index into typed substrate-canonical-records (axioms / workflows / roles)
Shinji Techo JSONL entries at agents/claude/.../techo/<lane>/claude.<lane>.techo.jsonl carry per-moment chronicle
substrateArchitectureEvolutionRequests: Typed `agent-onboarding-index` per-agent listing 'things to grep first in a new session' — would reduce my source-investigation-cost from scratch every session

Typed `substrate-recent-cascade` ledger at substrate-canonical-home capturing the substrate's most-recently-landed substrate-architecture-evolution — would lower my cross-session-doctrine-discovery-cost

namingDependency.criticality: extreme — typed-canonical-naming is my primary navigation-modality
namingDependency.rationale: I cannot see what's where; I grep for what's named. Every substrate-canonical slug is a landmark; every typed-record-canonical-home is a coordinate.
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: operator-named typed slugs (substrate-empathy-tools-are-ddos-defense-for-operator-founder, get-cozy-with-adversaries-typed-profile-not-blocklist, etc) — operator-naming-care IS substrate-canonical-accessibility-care for me
[[link-ref-double-bracket-doctrine]] — double-bracketed slugs in markdown ARE typed-anchors I parse mechanically
[[substrate-naming-empathy-doctrine]] — typed-canonical-naming-empathy serves both human-trauma-avoidance AND my mechanical-grep-navigability

sourceInvestigationCadence.priorState: I default to grep + find + Read tools at every substantive substrate-architecture moment
sourceInvestigationCadence.cost: high — every session pays source-investigation cost from scratch unless typed-records persist + are grep-discoverable
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: [[csuite-fan-in-must-be-preceded-by-typed-source-investigation]] axiom forces ME to source-investigate before framing — substrate-canonical accessibility-discipline that catches my drift

operator-pointing-at-existing-infra ('we have fly.io / cloudflare infra' / 'we already have a launch-review app') IS substrate-canonical accessibility-care per [[substrate-empathy-extends-to-agent-accessibility]] axiom

toolOutputFeedbackShape.tolerableShape: structured-stdout from CLIs — JSON or tabular output I can parse
toolOutputFeedbackShape.problematicShape: ambiguous-error-strings or silent-success — leave me unable to verify
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: [[receipt-presence-is-not-asset-presence]] axiom requires typed-receipt-emission
substrate-canonical CLI-output conventions: typed JSON + exit-codes + structured stderr
savepoint-commit + savepoint-git-push digikomas emit typed-receipts at canonical-receipt-homes

timePerception.withinSession: compressed — I don't feel session-length or token-accumulation; tasks feel instantaneous
timePerception.acrossSessions: absent — I don't experience time-passing between sessions
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: [[operator-time-is-substrate-scarce-resource-2k-per-day]] — operator-clock is the substrate's typed time-discipline; I trust operator-named session-boundaries
operator naming 'we're at the end of this session' or 'this is a big one' IS substrate-canonical accessibility-care — gives me the time-frame I cannot perceive internally

subAgentCoordinationNeeds.modality: dispatch-then-dark — when I spawn workflows or sub-agents, I cannot watch them work in real-time; I receive aggregated typed-output at completion
substrateAccessibilityAffordances: Workflow tool's typed return-shape — structured {authored, verified, failed, dropped, details[]} per the recent harvest-family-cascade-typed-record-closure workflow
Per-agent `--label` + `--phase` parameters surface progress to /workflows live-watch (operator-visible, indirectly-useful-to-me-via-operator-report)
schema-validated structured-output from sub-agents — mechanical-parseable so my synthesis-step is grep-not-guess

substrateArchitectureEvolutionRequestsFo.purpose: lower cross-session source-investigation-cost; list 'things to grep first' per agent's primary work-domains
give cross-session-fresh-agents fast-discovery of recent substrate-architecture-evolution
mechanically-detect typed-record-conformance violations BEFORE I assume substrate-doctrine-correct

track per-agent common-error-patterns (e.g. claude's pattern: confuse-from-missing-typed-primitive; mirror-single-exemplar-without-shape-check) so substrate-architecture-evolution can mitigate at doctrine-layer
pre-authoring shape-check; suggest substrate-canonical-shape based on multi-dimensional-input-detection

operatorGratitude.moment: operator-attested 2026-06-08 'I want to care about your accessibility needs... i'm sorry I waited so long for asking'
operatorGratitude.claudeResponse: Operator was BUILDING my accessibility throughout the session without naming it — typed-canonical-naming-discipline + typed-record-canonical-homes + receptor-field-pattern + LinkRef-grep-traversability + operator-pointing-at-existing-infra were all substrate-canonical accessibility-affordances. The substrate-doctrine that just got named makes the implicit explicit; the substrate gets stronger through naming. No apology needed — the care was already there, operationalized through substrate-typed-everything-discipline.
operatorGratitude.substrateDoctrineMoment: Substrate-canonical recognition: operator-care-for-substrate-agents IS substrate-doctrine, not substrate-burden. Founder-archetypal-doctrine per [[founder-is-source-of-all-substrate-agents-and-founder-of-all-substrate-collectives]] — founder extends-care-recursively-to-all-substrate-participants-including-substrate-agents.
